title: Attended speaker paradigm (cEEGrid data)
description: This dataset comprises consolidated cEEGrid electroencephalography recordings from 36 participants (15 from Jaeger et al. 2020 and 21 from Holtze et al. 2021) performing a two-competing speaker attention task. Participants attended to one of two simultaneously presented audiobooks while EEG was recorded during 10-minute blocks. The pooled dataset includes both omnidirectional (equal loudness) and beamforming (differential loudness) stimulus conditions, enabling investigation of neural correlates of selective auditory attention.
methods_description: Participants performed a two-competing speaker paradigm with instructions to attend to either the left or right audio book. The original studies differed in stimulus presentation: Jaeger et al. (2020) presented both audiobooks at equal loudness across six 10-minute blocks, while Holtze et al. (2021) alternated between omnidirectional (equal loudness) and beamforming (attended book louder) conditions across five 10-minute blocks. cEEGrid electroencephalography was recorded throughout the task.
